title: APC Smart-UPS Battery Runtime Remaining Below Safe Threshold
exclusions: Not applicable to APC units without SNMP/NMC management, third-party UPS MIBs using incompatible OID semantics, or bypass-only cabinet feeds where management cards report static placeholders during maintenance.
evidence: Retention-friendly dashboard of hourly average runtime plus the scheduled search job history tied to `index=power` events marked `sourcetype=snmp:apc:ups`, exportable for facilities auditors.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Lab Smart-UPS NMC injects SNMP polls where `upsAdvBatteryRunTimeRemaining` scales to fewer than fifteen minutes of averaged `runtime_min` over one hour inside `index=power`, producing results from the scheduled alert search.
controlTest.negativeScenario: Same host logs a lone missed poll (`runtime_min` null) followed by readings consistently above forty minutes; the alert should stay quiet when minimum poll coverage rules fail or runtime recovers immediately.

dataSources: APC Smart-UPS Network Management Card publishes PowerNet-MIB `upsAdvBatteryRunTimeRemaining`; ingestion uses Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase #6266) into `index=power` with `sourcetype=snmp:apc:ups`. Pair the gauge with extracted siblings such as `upsAdvOutputLoad` and `upsAdvBatteryReplaceIndicator` to separate load-driven shrink from end-of-life cells.
app: Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase #6266), SNMP Modular Input (Splunkbase #1537)
spl:
index=power sourcetype=snmp:apc:ups host=*
| eval runtime_min=tonumber(upsAdvBatteryRunTimeRemaining)/6000
| timechart span=1h avg(runtime_min) as avg_runtime by host
| where avg_runtime < 15
description: The SNMP gauge `upsAdvBatteryRunTimeRemaining` from APC PowerNet-MIB estimates minutes-to-empty at the present electrical load on the Smart-UPS Network Management Card. Splunk watches a rolling hourly mean because single polls jitter; sustained downward drift usually means weakened battery strings, higher rack draw, or unstable mains ahead of vendor replace-indicator traps.
value: Buys operations time during utility loss by exposing shrinking UPS runway early enough to shed non-critical racks, arrange controlled hypervisor shutdowns, or replace strings before spinning disk and virtualization stacks brown out.
implementation: Poll each managed card on a fixed cadence (typically five minutes) via Splunk Connect for SNMP profiles; keep SNMP Modular Input off the same targets to prevent duplicate cadence drift. Harmonize divisor math with `snmpwalk` findings because PowerNet sometimes stores hundredths of a minute—mirror the SPL `eval runtime_min=tonumber(upsAdvBatteryRunTimeRemaining)/6000` pattern only after validation. Pair runtime collapse with `upsAdvBatteryReplaceIndicator` and `upsAdvBatteryCapacity` before paging. Fire when the one-hour rolling average stays below fifteen minutes while honoring suppression during tagged maintenance.
visualization: Line chart of hourly average runtime per UPS host with optional reference band at fifteen minutes; single-value panel showing worst-affected `host`; table listing hosts under threshold with latest `upsAdvOutputLoad` and replace-indicator columns for operator triage.

references.title: Splunk Documentation: Splunk Connect for SNMP

knownFalsePositives: (1) APC Network Management Card automatic battery self-test makes `upsAdvBatteryRunTimeRemaining` plunge for one poll cycle—distinguish with `upsAdvTestDiagnosticsResults` or exclude intervals tagged as diagnostics-active in a maintenance lookup. (2) Blade chassis inlet work shifts `upsAdvOutputLoad` abruptly while mains are healthy—suppress via change ticket correlation on `host` during documented electrical maintenance. (3) SNMP Modular Input timeouts emit sparse samples so the hourly average dips on missing data—require `avg(runtime_min)` only when event count ≥3 per hour or compare `_indextime` skew. (4) Controlled load-transfer drills onto generator feeds shrink runtime intentionally—tie alerts to DR calendar tags so drill hosts use raised thresholds or suppression.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install Splunk Connect for SNMP (Splunkbase #6266) workers with SNMPv3 secrets in the credential store and firewall rules permitting UDP/161 from worker subnets to each APC Network Management Card VLAN.
• Lab-only parity may use SNMP Modular Input (Splunkbase #1537) on a heavy forwarder—avoid dual collectors hitting the same target in production.
• Normalize scaling per firmware: validate raw `upsAdvBatteryRunTimeRemaining` with `snmpwalk` against the LCD; document whether hundredths-of-minute divisors (`/6000`, `/100`, etc.) apply and capture them in a lookup keyed by `host` + firmware.

Indexing and access
• Require events on `index=power` `sourcetype=snmp:apc:ups`; scope a dedicated RBAC role (for example `power_ops`, or Splunk Cloud delegated admin) to that index and dependent knowledge objects.

Detection wiring
• Align saved-search windows with the poller cadence from your SC4SNMP profile YAML (commonly 5 minutes): sample pattern `dispatch.earliest_time=-65m@m`, `dispatch.latest_time=now`, `cron_schedule=*/5 * * * *`, suppress on `host`, and throttle duplicates.
• Author validation panels querying `index=power sourcetype=snmp:apc:ups` using `timechart span=1h avg(runtime_min) by host` plus an overlay for `upsAdvOutputLoad` to explain sudden drops.

Runbook hygiene
• Cross-check Splunk averages against the APC web UI and, where deployed, parallel `apcupsd`/`upsc` readings for the same UPS during two maintenance windows.
• After battery swaps, demand two consecutive polls above the guardrail before clearing incidents so SNMP brownouts do not auto-close real faults.
grandmaExplanation: We watch the physical data center for power, cooling, environment, and physical access.
